# zenfra_cloud_integration_attachment (Resource)

Attaches a cloud integration to a stack, granting the stack access to cloud credentials.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "zenfra_cloud_integration_attachment" "app_aws" {
  integration_id = data.zenfra_cloud_integration.aws_prod.id
  stack_id       = zenfra_stack.app.id
  read           = true
  write          = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `integration_id` (String) The cloud integration to attach.
- `stack_id` (String) The stack to attach the cloud integration to.

### Optional

- `read` (Boolean) Whether the attachment grants read access. Defaults to true.
- `write` (Boolean) Whether the attachment grants write access. Defaults to true.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the attachment was created.
- `external_id` (String) The external ID used for assuming the cloud role.
- `id` (String) The attachment identifier.
- `is_auto_attached` (Boolean) Whether this attachment was automatically created.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using composite ID: integration_id:attachment_id
terraform import zenfra_cloud_integration_attachment.app_aws $INTEGRATION_ID:$ATTACHMENT_ID
```
